MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-20-2024 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-22-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David R. Shane, Esq., SHANE LAW (415) 464-2020 Attorney for Plaintiff Kevin Darvive, Via ZOOM

MARGUERITE MEADE, ESQ., O'HAGAN MEYER LLP (415) 578-6900 mmeade@ohaganmeyer.com Attorney for Defendant, TURK ST. BOOKS, INC.

Hearing is held. ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT TURK ST. BOOKS, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.

Defendant's motion for summary judgment or, in the alternative, summary adjudication is denied. Defendant failed to maintain its burden of production per CCP 437c(p)(2).

In determining this motion, the court strictly construes defendant's evidence while liberally construing plaintiff's. (Fernandez v. Alexander (2019) 31 Cal.App.5th 770, 779 ["The declarations in support of a motion for summary judgment should be strictly construed, while the opposing declarations should be liberally construed."].) "All doubts as to whether there are any triable issues of fact are to be resolved in favor of the party opposing summary judgment." (Ingham v. Luxor Cab Co. (2001) 93 Cal.App.4th 1045, 1049.)

"The theory underlying a warning defect cause of action is that the product is dangerous because it lacks adequate warnings or instructions. [citation] 'Generally speaking, manufacturers have a duty to warn consumers about the hazards inherent in their products. [Citation.] The requirement's purpose is to inform consumers about a product's hazards and faults of which they are unaware, so that they can refrain from using the product altogether or evade the danger by careful use' 'Negligence law in a failure-to-warn case requires a plaintiff to prove that a manufacturer or distributor did not warn of a particular risk for reasons which fell below the acceptable standard of care, i.e., what a reasonably prudent manufacturer would have known and warned about.'" (Chavez v. Glock, Inc. (2012) 207 Cal.App.4th 1283, 1304-1305.)

"There is no duty to warn of known risks or obvious dangers." (Id. at 1304.) "California law also recognizes the obvious danger rule, which provides that there is no need to warn of known risks under either a negligence or strict liability theory." (Johnson v. American Standard, Inc. (2008) 43 Cal.4th 56, 67.) In addition, "if the product's manufacturer could foresee the misuse, the manufacturer remains liable unless it provides an adequate warning. A manufacturer is required to foresee some degree of misuse and abuse of a product and to take reasonable precautions to minimize the resulting harm". (Bunch v. Hoffinger Industries, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1278, 1302-1303.

In this case, whether the warning (or lack thereof) was adequate, the danger obvious, or the misuse foreseeable raises factual issues. (Bunch v. Hoffinger Industries, Inc. (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 1278, 1302-1303 [the adequacy of a warning label is an issue of fact for the trier of fact]; Torres v. Xomox Corp. (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1, 19 ["[F]oreseeability is a question for the jury unless undisputed facts leave no room for a reasonable difference of opinion."].) The evidence discloses that plaintiff purchased the pill from defendant and the clear plastic casing that housed the pill was similar in shape. (UMF 8; Defendant's Exs. I, J, K.) Based on the adduced pictures, the trier of fact could conclude that it was unclear whether there was a pill within the casing and the defendant's failure to warn plaintiff to remove the casing caused plaintiff's injury.

The court need not rule on defendant's objections to the Phiroz declaration since the court need not rely on that evidence to dispose of the motion. (CCP 437c(q).)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/RBU)
